items.tikz_code:
% Define vertices of equilateral triangle with side length 5
% Place base R and S on x-axis, T above
\tkzDefPoint(0,0){R}
\tkzDefPoint(5,0){S}
\tkzDefPoint(2.5,4.330){T}   % height = 5*sqrt(3)/2 ≈ 4.330

% Draw the triangle
\tkzDrawPolygon[thick](R,S,T)

% Mark all three sides with single tick marks to show equal lengths
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|, size=4pt](R,S)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|, size=4pt](S,T)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|, size=4pt](T,R)

% Label the side lengths
\tkzLabelSegment[below](R,S){$5$}
\tkzLabelSegment[right](S,T){$5$}
\tkzLabelSegment[left](T,R){$5$}

% Label the vertices
\tkzLabelPoints[below left](R)
\tkzLabelPoints[below right](S)
\tkzLabelPoints[above](T)

% Draw the points
\tkzDrawPoints[size=5](R,S,T)

items.tikz_code:
% Acute triangle JKL with angle K = 60°, angle L = 70°, angle J = 50°
% Place K at origin, L on x-axis
\tkzDefPoint(0,0){K}
\tkzDefPoint(4,0){L}

% J is determined by angles at K (60°) and L (70°)
% From K, ray at 60° above horizontal; from L, ray at 110° (180°-70°) above horizontal
% Intersection gives J
\tkzDefPoint(4,0){L}
\tkzDefShiftPoint[K](60:10){K2}   % direction from K
\tkzDefShiftPoint[L](110:10){L2}  % direction from L (180-70=110 degrees)
\tkzInterLL(K,K2)(L,L2)
\tkzGetPoint{J}

% Foot of altitude from J perpendicular to KL
\tkzDefPointBy[projection=onto K--L](J)
\tkzGetPoint{H}

% Draw the triangle
\tkzDrawPolygon[thick](J,K,L)

% Draw the altitude
\tkzDrawSegment[dashed, thick, blue](J,H)

% Mark the right angle at H
\tkzMarkRightAngle[size=0.22, color=blue](J,H,L)

% Mark angles at K and L
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.6, arc=l, color=orange](L,K,J)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85, color=orange](L,K,J){$60°$}

\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.6, arc=l, color=teal](J,L,K)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85, color=teal](J,L,K){$70°$}

% Mark angle at J
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.5, arc=l, color=purple](K,J,L)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.72, color=purple](K,J,L){$50°$}

% Define the base segment KL
\tkzDefPoint(0,0){K}
\tkzDefPoint(4,0){L}

% Construct triangle JKL with \angle K = 60° and \angle L = 70°
% From K, draw a ray making 60° with KL (counterclockwise)
\tkzDefPoint(2,0){T} % helper point on KL to set direction
\tkzDefPointBy[rotation=center K angle 60](T)\tkzGetPoint{Kdir}
\tkzDrawLine[add=0 and 2](K,Kdir)

% From L, draw a ray making 70° with LK (counterclockwise)
\tkzDefPointBy[rotation=center L angle 110](K)\tkzGetPoint{Ldir} % 180-70 = 110
\tkzDrawLine[add=0 and 2](L,Ldir)

% Intersection of the two rays is J
\tkzInterLL(K,Kdir)(L,Ldir)\tkzGetPoint{J}

% Draw the triangle
\tkzDrawPolygon(J,K,L)

% Drop the altitude from J to KL, foot H
\tkzDefLine[orthogonal=through J](K,L)\tkzGetPoint{Jh}
\tkzInterLL(J,Jh)(K,L)\tkzGetPoint{H}

% Draw the altitude JH
\tkzDrawSegment[dashed](J,H)

% Mark the right angle at H (angle KHL)
\tkzMarkRightAngle(K,H,L)

% Mark and label points

\tkzLabelPoints[below](K,L,H)

items.tikz_code:
% Triangle EFG: angle F = 60°, angle G = 70°, so angle E = 50°
% Place F at origin, G on x-axis
\tkzDefPoint(0,0){F}
\tkzDefPoint(5,0){G}

% Angle at F = 60°, so E is in direction 60° from F
% Angle at G = 70°, so E is in direction 180°-70°=110° from G
% Intersect the two rays to find E
\tkzDefPoint(1,1.732){F'} % direction 60° from F (unit step scaled)
\tkzDefPoint(4.658,0.940){G'} % direction 110° from G: cos110°≈-0.342, sin110°≈0.940 → (5-0.342,0.940)=(4.658,0.940)
\tkzInterLL(F,F')(G,G') \tkzGetPoint{E}

% Angle bisector from E: bisects angle FEG (angle E = 50°, half = 25°)
% D is on FG, found by angle bisector theorem: FD/DG = EF/EG
% Use tkz-euclide bisector approach via intersection
% Bisect angle FEG → get bisector ray, intersect with FG
\tkzDefLine[bisector](F,E,G) \tkzGetPoint{E'}
\tkzInterLL(E,E')(F,G) \tkzGetPoint{D}

% Draw the triangle
\tkzDrawPolygon[thick](E,F,G)

% Draw the angle bisector
\tkzDrawSegment[dashed, thick, blue](E,D)

% Mark the two equal half-angles at E
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.55, arc=l, color=red](F,E,D)
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.75, arc=l, color=red](D,E,G)

% Mark angles at F and G
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.6](G,F,E)
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.6](E,G,F)

% Label angles at F and G
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.9](G,F,E){$60°$}
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.95](E,G,F){$70°$}

% Label angle at E
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=1.25, color=red](F,E,D){$25°$}
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=1.45, color=red](D,E,G){$25°$}

% Label points
\tkzLabelPoints[above](E)
\tkzLabelPoints[below left](F)
\tkzLabelPoints[below right](G)
\tkzLabelPoints[below](D)

% Draw points
\tkzDrawPoints(E,F,G,D)

Draw a triangle EFG with angle F = 60° and angle G = 70°, together with its inscribed circle (the largest circle that fits inside). Label the incenter I and all three vertices.

items.tikz_code:
% All key coordinates are pre-computed via tkz-elements Lua below.
% Triangle EFG: F=(0,0), G=(4,0), E computed from angles.
% Angles: E=50 deg, F=60 deg, G=70 deg.
% Using law of sines with FG=4:
%   FG/sin(E) = EF/sin(G) = EG/sin(F)
%   FG/sin(50) = 4/0.766 = 5.222
%   EF = 5.222*sin(70) = 4.906   (side opposite G, i.e., EF)
%   EG = 5.222*sin(60) = 4.523   (side opposite F, i.e., EG)
% E from F at angle 60 deg, distance EF=4.906:
%   E = F + EF*(cos60, sin60) = (0,0)+(4.906*0.5, 4.906*0.866) = (2.453, 4.249)
% Incenter = (a*E + b*F + c*G)/(a+b+c)
%   a = side opposite E = FG = 4
%   b = side opposite F = EG = 4.523
%   c = side opposite G = EF = 4.906
%   sum = 13.429
%   Ix = (4*2.453 + 4.523*0 + 4.906*4)/13.429 = (9.812 + 0 + 19.624)/13.429 = 29.436/13.429 = 2.192
%   Iy = (4*4.249 + 4.523*0 + 4.906*0)/13.429 = 16.996/13.429 = 1.266
% Inradius = Area / s, where s = perimeter/2 = 13.429/2 = 6.715
%   Area = 0.5 * base * height = 0.5 * 4 * 4.249 = 8.498
%   r = 8.498 / 6.715 = 1.265

\tkzDefPoint(0,0){F}
\tkzDefPoint(4,0){G}
\tkzDefPoint(2.453,4.249){E}
\tkzDefPoint(2.192,1.265){I}
% Foot of incircle on FG is directly below I (since FG is on x-axis)
\tkzDefPoint(2.192,0){Ifoot}

% === Triangle XYZ with angles Y=60°, Z=70°, so X=50° ===
% Place Y at origin, Z on x-axis, compute X by law of sines.
% Let YZ = 5 cm (base). Then:
%   x/sin(X) = YZ/sin(X)  -- actually use law of sines properly
%   angle X = 50°, Y = 60°, Z = 70°
%   By law of sines: YZ/sin(X) = XZ/sin(Y) = XY/sin(Z)
%   YZ = 5, sin(50°)≈0.7660, sin(60°)≈0.8660, sin(70°)≈0.9397
%   XZ = 5*sin(60°)/sin(50°) ≈ 5*0.8660/0.7660 ≈ 5.652
%   XY = 5*sin(70°)/sin(50°) ≈ 5*0.9397/0.7660 ≈ 6.134
%
% Y = (0,0), Z = (5,0)
% X: angle at Y = 60° → X is at angle 60° from YZ direction
%   X = (XY*cos(60°), XY*sin(60°)) = (6.134*0.5, 6.134*0.8660) ≈ (3.067, 5.312)

\tkzDefPoint(0,0){Y}
\tkzDefPoint(5,0){Z}
\tkzDefPoint(3.067,5.312){X}

% Midpoints
\tkzDefMidPoint(Y,Z) \tkzGetPoint{D}   % midpoint of YZ
\tkzDefMidPoint(Z,X) \tkzGetPoint{E}   % midpoint of ZX
\tkzDefMidPoint(X,Y) \tkzGetPoint{F}   % midpoint of XY

% Centroid G = intersection of any two medians
\tkzInterLL(X,D)(Y,E) \tkzGetPoint{G}

% Draw the main triangle
\tkzDrawPolygon[thick, blue!70!black](X,Y,Z)

% Draw the medial triangle (dashed)
\tkzDrawPolygon[dashed, gray!60](D,E,F)

% Draw the three medians
\tkzDrawSegment[red!70!black, thick](X,D)
\tkzDrawSegment[red!70!black, thick](Y,E)
\tkzDrawSegment[red!70!black, thick](Z,F)

% Mark equal segments on YZ (D is midpoint)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|,  thick](Y,D)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|,  thick](D,Z)

% Mark equal segments on ZX (E is midpoint)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=||, thick](Z,E)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=||, thick](E,X)

% Mark equal segments on XY (F is midpoint)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|||,thick](X,F)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|||,thick](F,Y)

% Mark angles Y and Z
\tkzMarkAngle[arc=l, size=0.55, color=blue!60](Z,Y,X)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85](Z,Y,X){$60°$}

\tkzMarkAngle[arc=l, size=0.55, color=blue!60](X,Z,Y)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85](X,Z,Y){$70°$}

\tkzMarkAngle[arc=l, size=0.55, color=blue!60](Y,X,Z)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85](Y,X,Z){$50°$}

% Draw and label the centroid G
\tkzDrawPoint[size=5, color=red!80!black](G)
\tkzLabelPoint[right](G){$G$}

% Label vertices
\tkzLabelPoint[below left](Y){$Y$}
\tkzLabelPoint[below right](Z){$Z$}
\tkzLabelPoint[above](X){$X$}

% Label midpoints

% === Triangle MNO ===
% Angle N = 50°, Angle O = 80°, Angle M = 50°
% Place N=(0,0), O=(4,0). Side NO = 4.
% By sine rule: NO/sin(M) = MN/sin(O) = MO/sin(N)
% sin(50°)≈0.7660, sin(80°)≈0.9848
% MN = 4*sin(80°)/sin(50°) ≈ 5.143  (but we'll use computed coords)
% M is located from N at angle 50° and from O at angle 180°-80°=100°
% Mx = NO * sin(O)*cos(N_angle) ... use direct trig:
%   From N=(0,0), direction 50° above x-axis
%   From O=(4,0), direction 180°-80°=100° from positive x-axis
% M = intersection: parametric
%   N + t*(cos50, sin50) = O + s*(cos100, sin100)
%   t*cos50 = 4 + s*cos100
%   t*sin50 = s*sin100
% => t = 4*sin80 / sin50  ... wait, standard formula:
%   t = 4*sin(80°)/sin(50°) ≈ 5.143  -- this is length MN? No, t is param
% Actually M_x = t*cos(50°), M_y = t*sin(50°)
% t*sin(50) = s*sin(100) and t*cos(50)-s*cos(100)=4
% From eq1: s = t*sin(50)/sin(100)
% Sub: t*cos(50) - t*sin(50)*cos(100)/sin(100) = 4
% t*(cos50*sin100 - sin50*cos100)/sin100 = 4
% t*sin(100-50)/sin100 = 4  => t*sin50/sin100 = 4
% t = 4*sin(100)/sin(50) = 4*0.9848/0.7660 ≈ 5.143
% Mx = 5.143*cos(50°) ≈ 5.143*0.6428 ≈ 3.306 -- wait let me redo
% t = 4*sin(80°)/sin(50°)  -- sin(100)=sin(80)
% t = 4*0.9848/0.7660 ≈ 5.143
% Mx = 5.143 * cos(50°) = 5.143 * 0.6428 ≈ 3.306 ... hmm close to O
% My = 5.143 * sin(50°) = 5.143 * 0.7660 ≈ 3.940
% Scale down: use NO=4, M≈(3.306, 3.940) -- that's too tall, scale by 0.9
% N=(0,0), O=(3.6,0), M=(2.975, 3.546) -- let's just use NO=3.5
% Rescale everything by 3.5/4=0.875:
% N=(0,0), O=(3.5,0), M=(3.306*0.875, 3.940*0.875)=(2.893,3.447)
% Midpoints: D=mid(NO)=(1.75,0), E=mid(OM)=((3.5+2.893)/2,(0+3.447)/2)=(3.197,1.724)
%            F=mid(MN)=((2.893+0)/2,(3.447+0)/2)=(1.447,1.724)

\tkzDefPoint(0,0){N}
\tkzDefPoint(3.5,0){O}
\tkzDefPoint(2.893,3.447){M}

% Midpoints
\tkzDefMidPoint(N,O) \tkzGetPoint{D}
\tkzDefMidPoint(O,M) \tkzGetPoint{E}
\tkzDefMidPoint(M,N) \tkzGetPoint{F}

% Draw outer triangle MNO
\tkzDrawPolygon[thick, blue!70!black](M,N,O)

% Draw inner medial triangle DEF
\tkzDrawPolygon[thick, red!70!black](D,E,F)

% Draw tick marks on sides to show midpoints
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|, size=4pt](N,D)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|, size=4pt](D,O)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=||, size=4pt](O,E)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=||, size=4pt](E,M)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|||, size=4pt](M,F)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=|||, size=4pt](F,N)

% Mark angles
\tkzMarkAngle[arc=l, size=0.55, color=blue!60](O,N,M)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85](O,N,M){$50°$}

\tkzMarkAngle[arc=l, size=0.55, color=blue!60](M,O,N)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85](M,O,N){$80°$}

\tkzMarkAngle[arc=l, size=0.55, color=blue!60](N,M,O)
\tkzLabelAngle[pos=0.85](N,M,O){$50°$}

% Label outer vertices
\tkzLabelPoint[left](N){$N$}
\tkzLabelPoint[right](O){$O$}
\tkzLabelPoint[above](M){$M$}

% Label midpoints
\tkzLabelPoint[below](D){$D$}
\tkzLabelPoint[right](E){$E$}
\tkzLabelPoint[left](F){$F$}

% Draw dots at all points
\tkzDrawPoints[size=4, fill=blue!70!black, color=blue!70!black](M,N,O)
\tkzDrawPoints[size=4, fill=red!70!black, color=red!70!black](D,E,F)

% The projection formula: H = A + t*(B-A) where t = (C-A)·(B-A) / |B-A|²
% (C-A) = (3,4), (B-A) = (3,4)
% (C-A)·(B-A) = 9 + 16 = 25

% Parallel lines: first through W-G-X, second through Y-H-Z
% Transversal crosses first line at G, second line at H

% First parallel line (top)
\tkzDefPoint(0, 3){W}
\tkzDefPoint(6, 3){X}

% Second parallel line (bottom)
\tkzDefPoint(0, 0){Y}
\tkzDefPoint(6, 0){Z}

% Transversal: crosses top line at G and bottom line at H
% Angle of 40° with the horizontal
% Place G at x=2 on top line, H at x=2+3*tan(50°)≈2+3.577≈3.58 on bottom line
% (using 50° from vertical = 40° from horizontal going down-right)
\tkzDefPoint(2, 3){G}
\tkzDefPoint(3.58, 0){H}

% Extended transversal points (beyond the parallel lines)
\tkzDefPoint(1.42, 4.2){T1}   % above top line
\tkzDefPoint(4.16, -1.2){T2}  % below bottom line

% Draw the parallel lines
\tkzDrawLine[thick, add=0 and 0](W,X)
\tkzDrawLine[thick, add=0 and 0](Y,Z)

% Draw the transversal
\tkzDrawSegment[thick](T1,T2)

% Draw tick marks indicating parallel lines
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=>>](W,X)
\tkzMarkSegment[mark=>>](Y,Z)

% ---- Corresponding angles at G and H (both ABOVE-RIGHT) ----
% Angle at G: between ray G->X (rightward) and ray G->H (downward along transversal)
%   = 40° below the horizontal on the right side
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.55, arc=l, color=blue, fill=blue!20](X,G,T2)

% Angle at H: between ray H->Z (rightward) and ray H->T2 (downward along transversal)
%   same position relative to H — corresponding angle
\tkzMarkAngle[size=0.55, arc=l, color=blue, fill=blue!20](Z,H,T2)

% Label the 40° angle at G (corresponding angle label)
\tkzLabelAngle[dist=0.95, color=blue](X,G,T2){$40°$}
\tkzLabelAngle[dist=0.95, color=blue](Z,H,T2){$40°$}

% Label all six points
\tkzLabelPoints[left](W,Y)
\tkzLabelPoints[right](X,Z)
\tkzLabelPoints[above left](G)
\tkzLabelPoints[above left](H)

% Draw visible points
\tkzDrawPoints[size=4](W,X,Y,Z,G,H)
